title: oxalis phaseolifolia
intro: Oxalis phaseolifolia, also known as the False Sorrel or Wild Sorrel, is a perennial plant native to South America, belonging to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an antiseptic, and exhibit antioxidant and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Oxalis phaseolifolia include its potential to help treat urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other kidney problems, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Oxalis phaseolifolia can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, or as a tincture by steeping the plant in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin.
